source_text.text:
[PDF_PAGE 40 | score=3] Snmmon Earth Grue - Gnome that does not violate the dictates of its deity. This includes giving up its life in combat. Only one minion can serve a priest at a time. The material components are the priest's holy symbol and a large gem of at least 1,000 gp value, which must he sacrificed to the deity in the casting (and is destroyed in the process). Notes: Granted hy the Mulhorandi deities of the FORGOTTEN REALMS setting. .. Divine Minion: AC -2: MV 12: HD 6; THACO 11;#AT3/2;Dmg ld12orbymeapon; SAAttack as 10th-level fighter, animal form; SD Save as . 10th -level fighter, immune to magic causing fear or doubt; MK 100/: SZ M 6'/2'; ML fearless (20); lnt high (13): %; AL as deity; XP 2,000. Thes
[PDF_PAGE 196 | score=3] Gravity Variation Fortify (Alteration) Sphere: War Level: 3 Range: 60 yds. Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 1 rd. Duration: Instantaneous Area ofEffect: IO-ft. cuhe/2 levels Saving Throw: None By means of this spell, the priest prepares an area as a defensive position. The fortify spell can be used to prepare an open outdoors area such as a field, road, or grassland, or a rough or broken out- dwrs area such as a hillside, forest, or boulder-fall. Large rooms or chambers such as a cavern or a great hall can he fortified as well. The exact effects of the spell depend on the nature ofthe site. A. Open Outdoor Site: A rampart or dike of earth and loose stone rises from the ground along the peri
[PDF_PAGE 200 | score=3] Tanglefoot \ c Shadow Engines (IllusionPhantasm) Sphere: War Level: 7 Range: 240 yards Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 3 tuns Duration: 8 turns Area of Effect: 180-yard X 180-yard square Saving Throw: None This spell creates the illusion of as many as four siege engines. The casting priest can choose from hallistae, siege towers, catapults, rams, or any com- bination thereof. Like the creatures created hy the spell shudow monsters, these illusory engines have at least a tenuous reality and can inflict damage on enemies. Illusory crews of the appropriate number and race accompany the shadow engines. The engines can move at a rate of 20 yards per turn and are unaf- fected by terrain consider
[PDF_PAGE 27 | score=2] Stone Walk Stone Walk (Alteration, Necromancy) Sphere: Elemental Earth Level: 7 Range: Special Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 1 rd. Duration: Special Area of Effect: Special Saving Throw: None This spell is used to link far-flung temples or sacred areas for important and emergency journeys. A stone walk is a special form of teleportation that circumvents all known magical harriers and safe- guards against teleportation, including gorgon's hloodproqfagainst teleportation spells, and so on. A stone walk involves two stony areas large enough to stand on. Both must he prepared with a secret ritual involving crushed gems before the spell itself is cast, Once the stone walk is cast, a being can
[PDF_PAGE 42 | score=2] Summon Shadow Spirit caster might summon Id4 giant ants by means of the spell, hut the possibility is only 30% unless giant ants are nearby. This spell does not function under water. The materials needed for this spell are the caster's holy symbol, a flower petal, and a bit of mud or wet clay. Notes: Common for &ids (PHE). Summon Lock Lurker (Evocation) Sphere: Summoning Level: 3 Range: 10 yds. Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 6 Duration: 8 hrs. Area of Effect: Special Saving Throw: None Within one round of casting this spell, the priest magically conjures Id3 lock lurkers. The lock lurk- ers appear in an area within spell range, as desired by the caster. They follow simple instructions of 
[PDF_PAGE 111 | score=2] Wandering Glyphs \ t IO-foot-wide by 140-foot-long wall to block a dun- geon passage, or any other sort of shape that suited his needs. The caster can also create a wall of 5-foot thickness, which inflicts half damage hut can be doubled in one of the other dimensions. Those with the ability to pass through overgrown areas are not hindered hy this harrier. The caster can dismiss the barrier on command. Notes: Common for druids (PHB). Wandering Glyphs (Abjuration, Evocation) Sphere: Ward Level: 5 Range: Touch Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 8 hrs. maximum Duration: Special Area of Effect: Special Saving Throw: Special This spell is cast to include two to six different surfaces located within
[PDF_PAGE 124 | score=2] Wealthword 3 fl wave rolls overland hut it causes no damage to swimmers on the open sea. Wave spells are unaffected by sea or weather con- ditions. Thus, a wave can he made to m at a right angle to or completely counter to the direction of any other waves. Wave spells affect the surface of the water only and have uo more affect on underwater creatures than naturally occurring waves. Notes: Uncommon for Sea priests; otherwise, very rare. Wave of Telekinesis - Gnome (Alteration) Sphere: Combat Level 4 Range: 0 Components: S Casting Time: 7 Duration: Instantaneous Area of Effect: 10-fi. wide path, 10 ft./level long Saving Throw: Neg. By means of this spell, the priest can hurl one or more objec
[PDF_PAGE 203 | score=2] reverie. The DM may rule that specific ceremonies he carried out hy the priest during the time of med- itation and the time of the granting of the spell. These ceremonies should he determined in accor- dance with the nature of the religion. The priest may he required to he in a major church or temple for the ceremony. The presence of junior priests and acolytes, perhaps united in mass prayer, may also he needed. However, these are only suggestions and should not he rigidly enforced-a god of travelers would not require a quest spell to be granted in a temple, for example. Introducing the Quest Spell Bringing a quest spell into a campaign should he a major event. It should create a powerfa1 at
